name: SearchWrapper
source_code: // src/components/SearchWrapper.astro --- import { getCollection } from 'astro:content' import SearchInput from './SearchInput.astro' import SearchResults from './SearchResults.astro' interface Result { category: string components?: number description?: string slug: string terms: string[] title: string } const [application, marketing, neobrutalism, blog] = await Promise.all([ getCollection('application'), getCollection('marketing'), getCollection('neobrutalism'), getCollection('blog'), ]) const allCollections = [...application, ...marketing, ...neobrutalism] .map(({ data }) => data) .sort((a, b) => a.title.localeCompare(b.title)) const collections: Result[] = allCollections.map( ({ category, components, description, slug, terms, title }) => { const componentCount = components.length + components.filter(({ dark }) => !!dark).length return { category, components: componentCount, description, slug: `/components/${category}/${slug}`, terms, title, } }, ) const posts: Result[] = blog.map(({ data }) => { return { category: '', description: data.description, slug: `/blog/${data.slug}`, terms: data.terms, title: data.title, } }) ---

visual_summary: SearchWrapper is a container component that holds a search input field and the search results. It is used in applications where a user needs to search for content and view the matching results. Developers would use it to add search functionality to their application.
generation_prompt:
Create a production-ready other component with the following characteristics:

Name: SearchWrapper
Description: SearchWrapper is a container component that holds a search input field and the search results. It is used in applications where a user needs to search for content and view the matching results. Developers would use it to add search functionality to their application.
Style: light, minimal
Interactions: no animation, no hover effects
Tags: search input, search results, container

Requirements:
- Works in React with TypeScript by default but adaptable to Vue, Svelte, or plain HTML/CSS
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Fully accessible with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
- Responsive and mobile-friendly
- No external dependencies beyond React and Tailwind unless necessary
- Include all necessary imports
- Export as default with sensible prop types

Generate clean production-ready code only. No explanations.
Reference implementation (adapt don't copy):
